reddit_title: Got hit by a commercial truck in Houston — do I actually need a specialized truck accident lawyer or will any personal injury attorney do?
reddit_body:
Truck accident cases in Texas are genuinely different from regular car accident claims, and it's worth understanding why before you hire just any PI attorney. Commercial trucks are regulated by the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA), meaning there are federal rules around driver hours, maintenance logs, and cargo loading that can all become evidence in your case. Trucking companies also typically involve multiple liable parties — the driver, the carrier, a leasing company, a shipper — which adds layers of complexity most general PI lawyers don't deal with regularly.

One of the biggest time-sensitive issues is evidence preservation. Black box data, dashcam footage, driver logs, and inspection records can be overwritten or destroyed quickly. An experienced truck accident attorney will often send a spoliation letter within the first few days to legally compel the company to preserve that data. If your lawyer doesn't know to do this immediately, you could lose critical evidence.

If you're in Houston and trying to figure out where to start, a free service like wreckmatch.com can match you with attorneys who specifically handle commercial truck cases in Texas — no cost, no obligation, just a way to get connected with someone who knows this area of law. Whatever you do, don't wait too long. The trucking company's insurer is already working the case.
